detection_signals: All Port wines must age for 10 years in barrel
Vintage Port and Tawny Port are aged the same way
Every Port wine is aged for a minimum of 20 years before release
why_incorrect: Port production involves multiple distinct styles with very different ageing protocols. Vintage Ports age briefly in barrel (2-3 years), then in bottle. Tawny Ports age in wooden barrels (10, 20, 30, or 40+ years).
misconception: All Port wines are aged in wooden barrels for a fixed minimum period (e.g., 10 years) before release.
corrected_understanding: Port styles have different ageing requirements: Vintage Port ages briefly in barrel then in bottle; Tawny Port ages in wooden casks for specified periods.
